howItWorks.intro: This uk stamp duty calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: SDLT is charged on price bands: 0% up to £250k, 5% £250k-£925k, 10% £925k-£1.5m, 12% over £1.5m
First-time buyer relief: 0% up to £425k, 5% £425k-£625k (properties up to £625k only)
Additional property surcharge: +3% on entire price
Effective rate = Total SDLT / Purchase Price x 100
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What are the current UK Stamp Duty rates?
Do first-time buyers get a Stamp Duty discount?
faq.answer: Yes. First-time buyers pay no Stamp Duty on properties up to £425,000 and 5% on the portion between £425,001 and £625,000. Above £625,000, standard rates apply.

faq.question: What is the additional property surcharge?
faq.answer: If you already own a property and buy another (e.g., buy-to-let or second home), you pay an extra 3% surcharge on each band on top of the standard SDLT rates.

faq.question: When must Stamp Duty be paid?
faq.answer: Stamp Duty must be paid within 14 days of completion. Your solicitor or conveyancer typically handles the payment and SDLT return filing with HMRC.

sections.title: What Is the UK Stamp Duty Calculator?
sections.body: The UK Stamp Duty Calculator helps you get precise numbers for your specific situation in seconds. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Understanding the UK System
sections.body: UK financial regulations include specific thresholds, bands, and allowances that change with each tax year. This calculator reflects current UK rates so your results are accurate and actionable for planning purposes.
